items.title: Three years of the West Bank’s ‘third Nakba’
items.summary: Displacement and violence haunt Palestinian Bedouins, forced from their homes repeatedly since the Nakba of 1948.

items.title: Trump and Xi move towards business-first relationship after Beijing summit
items.summary: The two leaders did not resolve differences on key issues like Iran and Taiwan, despite push to extend truce.

items.title: How Xi-Trump summit failed to yield Iran war breakthrough
items.summary: US officials have nudged China to do more on the blocked Hormuz strait. President Xi doesn't appear to have budged.

items.title: Xi praises US ‘milestone’ visit and relationship, offers Trump roses
items.summary: Xi praised the new 'constructive and strategic' relationship with the US, before Trump concluded his China trip.

items.title: US seeks indictment of former Cuban leader Raul Castro
items.summary: The potential charges relate to Cuba's 1996 downing of humanitarian planes, US media outlets report.

items.title: Nadim Bawalsa & the Palestinian diaspora: From denial to genocide
items.summary: Nadim Bawalsa traces how early Palestinian migrants in Latin America forged national identity long before 1948.

items.title: Scientists identify massive new dinosaur following Thailand dig
items.summary: Nagatitan chaiyaphumensis had enough unique features to be considered a new species.

items.title: Benched Mbappe says he’s fourth-choice forward at Real Madrid under Arbeloa
items.summary: Striker jeered by own fans as he came off bench in return from injury in 2-0 La Liga win ​over Real Oviedo.

items.title: Nakba Day: 78 years of ethnic cleansing
items.summary: On May 15, Palestinians across historic Palestine and the diaspora commemorate the ‘Nakba’ or the ‘catastrophe’ of 1948.

items.title: Millions of Palestinians mark 78 years since the Nakba
items.summary: Nakba commemorations serve as remembrance and a call for justice, self-determination and the right to return.

items.title: Mitoma fails to make Japan’s 2026 World Cup squad due to hamstring injury
items.summary: Monaco forward Takumi Minamino also misses out due to injury, but ex-Arsenal defender Takehiro Tomiyasu makes the cut.

items.title: How David Ben-Gurion got the Palestinians wrong in 1948
items.summary: The Palestinian question never disappeared despite Israel’s best efforts and worst brutality.

items.title: Iran war day 77: Trump, Xi discuss Hormuz as Tehran rallies BRICS
items.summary: The US and Chinese leaders agreed during talks that the Strait of Hormuz should remain open to ensure global supplies.

items.title: Why India’s RSS is lobbying the West amid attacks on minorities at home
items.summary: Hate speech against religious minorities in India rose last year. Experts say far-right RSS is doing 'damage control'.

items.title: Trump and Xi hold high-stakes talks in Beijing
items.summary: US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping are meeting for a second day in Beijing.

items.title: Top US admiral: Strikes severely degraded Iran’s military, defence
items.summary: Admiral Brad Cooper defended the results of the US-Israeli war on Iran, as lawmakers questioned him on Capitol Hill.

items.title: After Trump’s pledge to ‘open up’ China, low expectations for trade deal
items.summary: Analysts see only modest breakthrough in ties between world's two largest economies.

items.title: What happens to those who say no to fighting wars?
items.summary: Al Jazeera’s @avawarrinerr explains what it means for people who object to fighting in wars today.

items.title: Israel-Lebanon talks held in Washington as expiration of ceasefire nears
items.summary: Al Jazeera's Manuel Rapalo reports from Washington, where the first of two days of Lebanon-Israel talks concluded.

items.title: Eurovision braces for new protests over Israel’s participation
items.summary: Eurovision is heading towards its grand final, but organisers are bracing for more demonstrations against Israel.

items.title: Iran war live: China wants swift end to war, Israel escalates in Lebanon
items.summary: China's Foreign Ministry says window for talks must not be lost, Israel steps up assassination attacks in south Lebanon.

items.title: Explosions heard as mining groups stage antigovernment protest in Bolivia
items.summary: Protesters have demanded the resignation of President Rodrigo Paz, who was elected on a platform of economic reform.

items.title: UN aid convoy hit by drone strikes in Ukraine’s Kherson
items.summary: A UN humanitarian convoy delivering aid to the city of Kherson was hit twice by drones.

items.title: Cuba’s Diaz-Canel open to US aid amid worsening fuel crisis, blackouts
items.summary: The comments came as a US delegation led by CIA Director John Ratcliffe met Cuban officials in Havana on Thursday.

items.title: Closing arguments begin in Elon Musk’s landmark lawsuit against OpenAI
items.summary: Elon Musk accuses OpenAI of breaching charitable trust and prioritising profit over AI safety and nonprofit values.
